# PA-0007 — Adjudication Record

---

# Proposal

- **Proposal ID**: PA-0007
- **Date**: 2026-04-19
- **Source**: briefs/animalia-banditry.md

Verbatim user proposal:

> Banditry has been common in this world for as long as anyone remembers. Desperate or plain malicious groups of sentient inhabitants of this world (humans and sentient animal peoples alike) sometimes camp or settle areas of the wilderness away from popular roads, cities, and well-defended settlements. These groups of bandits sometimes occupy caves or even exploited ruins of the Makers Age. As bandits, these groups ambush travelers or even caravans if they are desperate enough or believe they can get away with it. Their goal is to steal or ransom. Methods vary: some bandit groups never kill to avoid being hunted down harder or due to some semblance of morality, while other bandit groups prefer to kill to avoid letting witnesses alive. In particularly isolated and poorly-defended areas of the world, some bandits have even raided settlements, to steal, rape, and kidnap people. In such cases, some groups of bandits keep prisoners as slave labor or sexual slaves. When the activities of some bandit groups become bad enough, settlements offer contracts to hunt down these groups and either kill them or bring them to justice.
>
> There are varying reasons why people raised in settlements end up opting for banditry: being pushed out due to criminal activity, debts, etc. Some opt for banditry after brutal winters kill family members or temporarily render their previous labor unprofitable. In rare cases, some entire settlements, weakened for any reason, are abandoned and the survivors either try to emigrate to other regions or settlements, or become outlaws.
>
> User intention: my intention with these new canon facts is to add more danger to the world, to show that some groups of people don't operate by the rules of settlements whether big or small, and to add more meaningful work for adventurers, warriors and other contractors.
>
> Desired dramatic effect: the world becomes more dangerous and darker due to the existence of various bandit groups. This isn't Disney pirates: the bandit groups can be ruthless, violate any commonly-accepted moral principles, and engage in forced slavery and rapes.

## User-Stated Constraints

- **Preferred scope**: continent-wide ("for as long as anyone remembers"); geographic distribution wherever wilderness + push-out populations + vulnerable targets coexist; worst-behaviors (raid/rape/slavery) explicitly in isolated/poorly-defended areas only.
- **Desired rarity**: gradient — most bands small and short-lived; worst-behavior subset local; settlements offer contracts when intolerable.
- **Dramatic purpose**: more danger, showing groups outside civic rules; more meaningful work for adventurers/warriors/contractors.
- **Revision appetite**: explicit that this is NOT Disney pirates; explicit endorsement of ruthless/amoral/slavery/rape content.
- **Other**: adventurer-stratum emphasis is a FLAG for AES-1 hero-drift risk; register discipline therefore required.

---

# Phase 0–11 Analysis

## Phase 0: Normalize the Proposal

### Statement
Endemic banditry in Animalia as a historical-process: pushed-out-of-civic-order groups of any species occupy wilderness and previously-accessed Maker-Age ruin-subset as shelter, ambush travelers or (in weak-charter regions) raid settlements; gradient from never-kill through kill-witnesses to raid-and-enslave; suppression via settlement-hired hunter-contractors. Formalizes what was already implicit in GEOGRAPHY §Predator Zones, CF-0017/CF-0024 cost clauses, and INSTITUTIONS §Military "suppress banditry" function.

### Underlying World-Change
Formalizes endemic-banditry-as-background-condition; adds bandit-hunter as CF-0024 contractor subtype; commits gradient (never-kill / kill-witnesses / raid-and-enslave) with scope stabilizer localizing the worst subset; adds stolen-goods fencing as 5th product-line on the canal-corridor illegitimate-service rail; creates hunter-contractor ≠ dragoon civic-doctrinal register.

### Canon Fact Type(s)
`historical_process` (single CF). Rejected: split into multiple CFs (kept integrity); move into Mystery Reserve (phenomenon is open-known); contested_canon (banditry is objective, civic orders treat it as real).

### Additional Domain Files Loaded
All 13 mandatory world files (CANON_LEDGER.md scanned via Grep for CF/CH id allocation); 6 critic sub-agents dispatched at Escalation Gate.

## Phase 1: Scope Detection

- **Geographic**: stated scope matches logical scope — endemic as long as wilderness + push-out populations + vulnerable targets coexist (continuously the case). Global category; raid-and-enslave subset local to isolated/weak-charter/demographically-fragile regions.
- **Temporal**: cyclical ("for as long as anyone remembers") with recruitment-feeder fluctuation across Layer-3/Layer-4 cohorts.
- **Social**: public category; specific camps hidden.
- **Visibility/Reproducibility**: public awareness high; individual-camp intelligence regional/local; "diffusion" is recruitment-feeder-driven, not reproducibility-driven (banditry isn't a learned craft — it's opportunistic social formation).
- **Institutional awareness**: high; bounty-contract practice formalized; civic-watch/militia/contractor suppression.
- **Diffusion risk**: LOW as craft-diffusion; stabilized by seasonal attrition, self-suppression of worst bands, geographic chokepoint bounded, finite recruitment feeders.

## Phase 2: Invariant Check

- **Classification**: compatible (no invariant revision required; compatible scope with repair).
- **Invariants tested**:
  - ONT-1: compatible (bandits are ordinary biological sentients);
  - ONT-2: compatible (bandits are not magical; firewall required: bandits cannot wield functional Maker-Age artifacts — CAU-1/SOC-4 structure holds);
  - ONT-3: compatible (multi-species bandit groups obey; rape-produced children breed true to victim's species);
  - CAU-1: compatible (artifact-cost invariant bars bandit artifact-use);
  - CAU-2/3: compatible (wards/contamination signals unchanged);
  - DIS-1/2/3: compatible;
  - SOC-1: REINFORCED (multispecies banditry and victim-and-perpetrator-both-multispecies);
  - SOC-2: compatible with FIREWALL (bandit rape is assault, NOT the public adult barter SOC-2 governs — categorical distinction);
  - SOC-3: DEFINED BY ITS OUTSIDE (bandits are definitionally outside coin-contract order; reinforces SOC-3);
  - SOC-4: compatible with FIREWALL (bandit-trafficked artifacts are SOC-4 violation on top of banditry; but bandits cannot WIELD artifacts by CAU-1/SOC-4 structure);
  - AES-1: compatible with REGISTER DISCIPLINE (bandit-hunting is dangerous labor, not heroic quest);
  - AES-2: REINFORCED (all 5 clusters acquire ordinary-life banditry texture);
  - AES-3: compatible with FIREWALL (magical-contamination and moral-contamination must remain distinct aesthetically).
- **Hard rejection triggers**: NONE. No invariant violated; no genre contract collapsed; no world-scale destabilization; no retroactive invalidation.
- **Mystery Reserve coverage check**: proposal is user-authored (not propose-new-canon-facts output); no self-trace to verify; Phase 2 independent test finds interactions with M-1, M-2, M-6, M-7, M-8, M-9, M-10, M-13 requiring firewall extensions + new M-14 per Rule 7.

## Phase 3: Underlying Capability / Constraint Analysis

- **Now-possible**: bandit-hunting stories; backstories for characters fallen into or escaped from banditry; antagonist factions of mundane-human-evil register; recovery-of-kidnapped plots; regional variance in bandit severity as polity-strength indicator; bandit-occupied ruin as dangerous-but-not-cosmic adventure site; runaway-to-bandits escape arc.
- **No-longer-assumed**: safe wilderness travel; empty settlements as benign; unvetted independent work near corridors; disappearances as always accidental.
- **More valuable**: armed escort specialists; intelligence networks; fortified inns; village militia; hunter-contractor as explicit wage-table category.
- **More feared**: unfamiliar wilderness groups; strangers asking about travel plans; empty stretches; returning home to raided village.
- **Politically useful**: "bandit threat" rhetoric for militarization (dangerous — must firewall against dragoon mission creep); mercenary-vs-bandit-adjacent distinction charged; bounty-execution-morality a reform-current clergy axis.

## Phase 4: Prerequisites and Bottlenecks

Prerequisites classified common/uncommon/rare:
- Wilderness / under-populated areas: common
- Desperate/displaced populations: common (varies by year)
- Access to weapons: common
- Travelers/caravans to prey on: common on trade routes
- Shelter (caves, ruins, abandoned settlements): common
- Absence of effective pursuing force: varies
- Leadership/organizational capacity: uncommon
- Black-market fence: uncommon
- Geography favorable to ambush: uncommon to common
- Winter resilience: uncommon (winter kills many bands)

Bottlenecks on worst-bandit behaviors:
- Settlement defensibility (most settlements credibly defended)
- Civic response (bounty contracts + militia mobilization)
- Geographic marginality (most world NOT under-policed wildland)
- Self-limiting brutality-gradient (more brutal → more aggressive suppression)
- Criminal network capacity (supervision/food/containment for captives is real logistical limit)

## Phase 5: Diffusion and Copycat Analysis

- **Primary feeders**: debtor exiles; criminal exiles; heartland winter-starved; sealed-quarter survivors; deserters/failed-militia; CF-0021 failed-kiln-explosion survivors; CF-0024 failed-reputation contractors; clan-exiles.
- **Secondary feeders**: orphaned/abandoned-village survivors.
- **Suppressors**: militia, civic watch, chartered mercenary companies, contractor-veterans under bounty, noble retinues, clan-guard, caravan-master self-defense, extraction-guild contractor-auxiliary, chartered watch (NOT dragoons — mission-scope firewall).
- **Skeptics**: reform-current clergy (due-process-vs-summary execution).
- **Profiteers**: bounty-contract adventurers; black-market fences (5th product-line); urban receivers; bardic performers of bandit cycles; weapons-smiths/armorers; ransom-brokers; sedentary ex-bandits as fences.
- **Victims**: travelers, caravans, isolated farmsteads, demographically-fragile settlements, enslaved captives.
- **Non-adopters**: civically-integrated households; populations with clan-welfare support; adopting-polity ward-resident families.

## Phase 6: Consequence Propagation (13/13 domains touched)

### First-Order
Rural households know safe roads; escort hire rises; travelers join caravans; lookout-and-silence-rules in exposed communities; tavernkeepers watch faces; stolen goods → black market; ransom cash flows; bounty-contract wages; arms/inn/fortification demand rises; civic-watch arrest warrants; bounty contracts formalized; common-pantheon road-god framing; fortification; hunter-hunting specialists; "bandit"/"reformed-bandit"/"bandit-hunter-veteran" social roles; kidnapping traumas; fortified inns/caravanserai/watchtowers; lone-travel discouraged in wildland; bandit-camp wildlife disturbance; banditry reviled; trade-tongue bandit vocabulary; bandit ballads stratify.

### Second-Order
Bandit-hunt drill; warn-rhymes; caravan-master cargo-refusals; stranger-vouching; stolen goods as 5th canal-corridor product-line; authentication-cartel pressure compounds; ransom-broker role emerges; regional economic drag; evolving case law (bounty liability, extradition, trial-vs-execute, slave-recovery); clergy bifurcation on bounty-execution; bandit-hunter veteran as new CF-0024 subtype; architectural register expansion; caravan-escort demand; scout-guide stratum; abandoned-settlement livestock feral; reintegration fraught; cleansing-rite; "going outlaw" euphemism; named cycles accumulate.

### Third-Order
World-equilibrium accepting background bandit-risk as part of hazardous existence; ordinary-work register integrating bandit-risk management; regions with severe activity experience recurrent economic drag; bandit-suppression becomes civic-budget line; canal travel bandit-safer in heartland; bandit-corridor zones become semi-wild zones; cross-species rape creates distinct pollution register; long memory of raided-villages in place-names.

## Escalation Gate

Triggers evaluated:
- Invariant revision required? NO.
- >3 domains touched? **YES** (13/13).
- New invariant-level rule? Soft yes (endemic-banditry as baseline world-condition).

**At least one trigger fires** → 6 parallel critic sub-agents dispatched (reports below under "Critic Reports" section).

## Phase 7: Counterfactual Pressure Test

**Question**: If banditry is endemic, why hasn't the world been overwhelmed by it?

**Ten stabilizers, each naming a concrete mechanism**:
1. Seasonal attrition — winter kills bandits through exposure, starvation, internal violence (named mechanism: winter-kill).
2. Brutality-gradient self-limiting — worst bands attract aggressive suppression (bounty-escalation ladder correlated with brutality).
3. Geographic containment — corridors stabilize in chokepoints bounded by civic population distribution.
4. Finite recruitment feeders — civic welfare (monastic clinic, clan hearth-care, CF-0031 contamination-registry where adopting) partial counter-feeder.
5. Internal band-mortality — no-charter-no-enforcement dispute collapse.
6. Civic-memory-based suppression priority — bardic cycles + Predator Zones mapping + reputation-based prioritization.
7. Logistical slave-holding limits — supervision/food/shelter/containment constraints prevent slave-barony scale.
8. Hunter-contractor market — active and self-stabilizing (paid out of settlement + estate + merchant treasuries).
9. CAU-1/SOC-4 artifact-power firewall — bandits cannot wield functional Maker-Age artifacts by invariant structure.
10. Guardian-wipe self-selection — CF-0029 eliminates bandits intruding on catastrophic-class guardian-active ruins.

**All stabilizers name concrete mechanisms; no hand-waves.** Phase 14a Test 8 PASS.

## Phase 8: Contradiction Classification

- **Hard contradictions**: NONE.
- **Soft conflicts** (seeds `required_updates`):
  - GEOGRAPHY "Bandit corridors" implicit-canon → explicit formalization-annotation
  - CF-0017 bardic-circuit → bandit-ballad/raid-lament subgenre extension
  - CF-0024 contractor stratum → bandit-hunter named subtype + three-tier skirmish-season taxonomy
  - CF-0026 principled-refuser → bounty-execution morality bifurcation
  - CF-0028 ruin-expedition → two-threat model + M-10 selection-bias deepening
  - CF-0033 dragoon mission-scope → negative-scope clause
  - INSTITUTIONS (7 subsections); ECONOMY (5 subsections); EVERYDAY_LIFE (5 clusters); OPEN_QUESTIONS (new); MYSTERY_RESERVE (M-1/M-2/M-6/M-7/M-8/M-9/M-10/M-13 extensions + new M-14)
- **Latent Burdens** (7 entries in CH-0008 `latent_burdens_introduced`):
  1. Inter-polity bandit-extradition as potential sixth structural axis
  2. Liberated-slave reintegration civic procedure
  3. Ransom-prisoner civic-registry status
  4. Clergy bounty-ethics magistrate case law
  5. Hunter-contractor licensing framework (per polity)
  6. Ransom-broker charter status (per polity)
  7. Bounty-hunter liability case law
- **Scope Drift Risk**: raid-and-enslave subset — managed via distribution-clause geographic stabilizer.
- **Tone/Thematic Mismatch**: managed via register discipline (Theme/Tone forbidden/required vocabulary).

## Phase 9: Repair Pass

### Options Considered

- Split into multiple CFs (banditry + bandit-hunting + raid-and-enslave-hazard): **DECLINED** per Phase 0 tie-break rubric. Sub-facts share the same underlying world-change; split would fragment integrity.
- Move into Mystery Reserve: **DECLINED**. Banditry is open-known, not bounded-unknown.
- Create NEW Mystery Reserve entry: **ADOPTED** — M-14 Bandit-Captain Mythography Reliability (Rule 7 obligation from Phase 9 for bounded-unknown manufactured by the proposal — bardic named-captain register generates contested historicity distinct from M-7 specialist-reputation register).
- Localize geographically: **ADOPTED** — distribution clause localizes worst-bandit subset to isolated / weak-charter / demographically-fragile regions.
- Add cost / side effects / bottlenecks: **ADOPTED** — Phase 7 stabilizers integrated into CF costs_and_limits.
- Shift to contested_belief: **DECLINED**. Banditry is objective, not belief-only.
- Heritable/narrow-group: **N/A**.

### Options Declined (and why)
- CF split: integrity preservation.
- Mystery Reserve placement: wrong category (open-known phenomenon).
- Contested-belief: world-level objective fact.

### Options Adopted (preserved vs sacrificed)
- Single CF with distribution-clause scope stabilizer: preserves dramatic intent + fact integrity; sacrifices finer-grained grep-searchability of enslave-subset.
- Distribution-localization of worst subset: preserves Rule 4 discipline; sacrifices nothing.
- Widow-of-the-raid absorbed into CF-0024 widow-of-the-job: preserves Rule 3 firewall; sacrifices visible 7th-stratum status.
- Hunter-contractor ≠ dragoon load-bearing: preserves M-13 contingency + Charter-Era balance; sacrifices narrative-simplicity.
- Register discipline committed: preserves AES-1/2/AES-3 + tone-contract; sacrifices romanticized-outlaw register.
- Five mod_history entries on CF-0017/CF-0024/CF-0026/CF-0028/CF-0033: preserves Rule 6 anti-silent-retcon; sacrifices nothing (required).
- New M-14: preserves Rule 7 + bounds mystery residue.
- Ten Phase 7 stabilizers: preserves Rule 3 anti-specialness-inflation; no hand-waves.

## Phase 10: Narrative and Thematic Fit

- **Deepens identity**: YES — adds pragmatic-medieval-realism texture complementing multispecies + magic-as-hazard departures. Hazardous + ordinary + dread-not-nihilistic tone reinforced.
- **Creates tensions**: YES — SOC-3 defined by its outside; Charter-Era retinue pattern stressed but charter-sound in bandit-defense; CF-0026 bifurcation on bounty-execution; community reintegration fraught.
- **Trivializes struggle**: NO, given register discipline.
- **Universalizes specialness**: NO — bandits are mundane (no magic, no chosen-antagonist status); worst behaviors localized.
- **Undermines mystery**: NO (with firewalls installed). M-10 selection-bias firewall load-bearing and explicit.
- **Enriches ordinary life or only exceptional scenes**: ENRICHES — all 5 clusters gain ordinary-life texture.
- **Creates story engines or clutter**: CREATES — ransom-negotiator / hunter-return / tenant-raid / clan-son-walks-south story engines.
- **Rule 7 collision check**: no unrepaired forbidden-answer collisions; M-10 firewall load-bearing; M-14 manufactures bounded-unknown per Rule 7 obligation.

- **Test 4** (Rule 5 / Phase 6 2nd+3rd-order consequences in CF or required_world_updates): **PASS** — all consequences reflected in CF visible_consequences (23 entries spanning ordinary life / institutions / economy / memory) plus concrete Phase 13a patches to 8 downstream files.
- **Test 5** (Rule 6 / retcon_policy_checks all true): **PASS** — all five checks true in CH-0008; formalization-of-implicit framing explicit in CF statement and CH summary.
- **Test 6** (Rule 7 / no unrepaired forbidden-answer collisions): **PASS** — M-10 selection-bias firewall explicit in CF costs_and_limits + MYSTERY_RESERVE patch adding disallowed cheap answer; all other MR collisions preserved via cross-application extensions; M-14 bounds residual mystery.
- **Test 7** (required_world_updates each has one-sentence summary + concrete patch): **PASS** — 8 files each have one-paragraph summary in Phase 15a deliverable summary (artifact 4) and concrete drafted patches applied.
- **Test 8** (Phase 7 stabilizers name concrete mechanisms): **PASS** — 10 stabilizers each names a concrete mechanism (winter-kill attrition, brutality-gradient self-limit ladder, geographic chokepoint constraint, finite feeders + civic-welfare counter-feeder, no-charter internal-dispute collapse, civic-memory-based suppression priority, logistical slave-containment limit, hunter-contractor market, CAU-1/SOC-4 invariant artifact-firewall, CF-0029 guardian-wipe selection).
- **Test 9** (verdict reasoning cites specific phase findings): **PASS** — verdict cites Phase 2 (invariant-safe), Phase 6 (13/13 domains), Phase 7 (10 stabilizers), Phase 8 (no hard contradictions; soft-conflicts resolved via updates), Phase 9 (scope stabilizer + M-14 + mod_history entries), Phase 10 (identity-deepening), Phase 6b (6-critic convergences).

**All 9 tests PASS. Phase 15a commit proceeded on user approval.**

---

# Verdict

**Verdict**: ACCEPT_WITH_REQUIRED_UPDATES

---

# Justification

The proposal is invariant-safe (Phase 2 — every invariant tested; none require revision; SOC-1 reinforced, SOC-3 defined-by-its-outside, AES-1/2/3 preserved via committed register discipline, CAU-1/SOC-4 structurally firewall bandits from functional artifact-use); dramatically rich (Phase 10 — deepens identity, creates productive tensions across Charter-Era retinue pattern / CF-0026 clergy bifurcation / kinship reintegration, enriches ordinary life across all 5 EVERYDAY_LIFE clusters, creates engine-coherent story seeds); and scope-manageable via distribution-clause localization of the worst-bandit subset (Phase 1 scope detection + Phase 9 repair — raid-and-enslave concentrated in isolated / weak-charter / demographically-fragile regions, NOT continent-wide). Consequences propagate across 13/13 domains (Phase 6), which fired the Escalation Gate and produced six-critic synthesis (Phase 6b) whose convergences informed all load-bearing commitments. Ten concrete stabilizer mechanisms named (Phase 7, no hand-waves). No hard contradictions (Phase 8); soft conflicts resolved via 5 modification_history entries on entangled CFs and 8 downstream-file patches. Rule 7 mystery-reserve obligations discharged via 8 firewall extensions (M-1, M-2, M-6, M-7, M-8, M-9, M-10 load-bearing-novel, M-13 cross-reference) and new M-14 entry bounding the residual bardic-captain-mythography unknown the proposal manufactures. Register discipline committed per Theme/Tone critic (forbidden/required vocabulary lists attached to CF notes; EVERYDAY_LIFE patches lead with ordinary-household register per AES-1/2 discipline; rape/enslavement content rendered in aftermath-in-household-vocabulary, never event-in-prose). The ACCEPT is UPDATES-conditioned because the proposal is dense (13/13 domain touch; 8 file patches; 5 CF mod_history entries; 8 MR extensions + M-14; new OPEN_QUESTIONS section) and bare ACCEPT would not carry the integration load.

---

# Critic Reports

## Continuity Archivist

### 1. Direct Contradictions

**None found.** Banditry is compatible with every invariant and CF record reviewed. The proposal formalizes what existing canon already presumes.

### 2. Soft Conflicts and Required Annotations

**CRITICAL — Rule 6 (No Silent Retcons) exposure**: GEOGRAPHY.md already names "Bandit corridors" as a Predator-Zone subcategory; CF-0017 *costs_and_limits* names "bandit corridors" as a bardic-travel hazard; CF-0024 *costs_and_limits* names "escort-gap bandit losses." INSTITUTIONS.md Military/Defense names "suppress banditry" as a function. **The proposal must explicitly state it is formalizing and extending these pre-existing implicit references, not introducing banditry de novo.** The CF statement and change log summary must both name this continuity. A silent introduction would constitute a Rule 6 violation by retroactively reading prior cosmetic mentions as foundation-less.

**CF-0017** — bandit-ballad/raid-lament subgenre extension parallels the CF-0024 extension already logged; requires `modification_history` entry noting the bardic-circuit subgenre expansion and the performer-martyr class (bards killed while traveling corridors).

**CF-0024** — bandit-hunting becomes a named fifth contractor subtype alongside local-warrior/bard-agent/extraction-escort/caravan-wildlife-escort/mercenary-band. Because CF-0024's statement enumerates subtypes explicitly, this **requires a `modification_history` entry** (scope-extension, not silent re-reading). "Escort-gap bandit losses" is preserved but the subtype enumeration changes.

**CF-0025** — no modification needed; bandit-hunter and fallout-specialist are distinct career tracks. Overlap is reputational (scarred veterans) not functional. The CF-0025 *empirical-not-metaphysical* firewall structure is a pattern worth replicating for bandit-hunter reputation.

**CF-0026** — new bounty-execution morality split is real and belongs in a `modification_history` entry. Principled-refusers already refuse *artifact-adjacent* work; bandit-hunting-with-killing is a distinct ethical axis. Caveat: CF-0026 already notes refusers "often take … mundane skirmish mercenary work" — so bandit-hunting-as-non-artifact-work is partially covered. Only the bounty-execution (deliberate killing under warrant) sub-question is new.

**CF-0028** — bandits occupying non-guardian enterable ruins is a real interaction: requires `modification_history` noting that expedition security now faces a two-threat model (guardian wipes + bandit resistance), and that the M-10 selection-bias is *deepened* (bandit-occupation preferentially selects guardian-less sites — bandits who occupy guardian-active sites are dead).

**CF-0029** — no modification needed. Guardian wipes of intrusive bandits extend M-8 bardic tragic-cycle material without crossing the M-8 firewall (bandits don't study mechanism).

**CF-0033** — **mission-scope firewall must be written into the new CF's *costs_and_limits***: dragoons do NOT pursue banditry; bandit-pursuit is contractor/militia/levy work. This is load-bearing for Stabilizer-1 (anti-monopoly political cost).

**CF-0008** — bandit artifact-trafficking is a SOC-4 aggravated offense (banditry + unlicensed artifact possession). Annotation only, not modification.

### 3. Required Updates — Concrete Prose

**CANON_LEDGER.md change-log entry** must read: "formalization of pre-existing endemic banditry implicit in GEOGRAPHY.md Predator Zones, CF-0017/CF-0024 cost clauses, and INSTITUTIONS.md Military/Defense 'suppress banditry' function."

**New CF scope**: `geographic: global`, `temporal: ongoing`, `social: public` for the category / `restricted_group` for specific camps.

**Worst-bandit subset** (raid+slavery+rape) must be a *distribution* clause with `where_concentrated: isolated/weak-charter areas`, NOT a separate CF, to prevent globalization-by-accident.

### 4. Critical Risks

1. **Rule 6 silent-retcon via GEOGRAPHY.md** if formalization-framing is omitted (highest risk).
2. **CF-0033 mission-creep** if dragoons-vs-bandits boundary not explicitly firewalled.
3. **Globalization-by-accident (Rule 4)** if slavery/rape raid-subset isn't scope-narrowed to weak-charter regions.
4. **Latent M-10 interaction** — bandit occupation of ruins empirically confirms guardian-less-site selection; must be framed as selection-bias reading, NOT as Maker-cosmology evidence.

### Timeline Updates Required
- **Layer 1 (Maker Age)**: NO update.
- **Layer 2 (Charter Era)**: brief annotation (Charter-Era settlement pushed banditry to wilderness margins).
- **Layer 3 (Incident Wave)**: annotation (bad-winter and sealed-quarter cohorts drove recruitment spikes).
- **Layer 4 (Current)**: new bullet (bad winter-fever years + canal-displaced + sealed-quarter refugees feed documented recent uptick; bandit-hunting contracts multiply on tavern boards).

### Latent Burdens
1. Inter-polity bandit-extradition (potential sixth axis).
2. Liberated-slave reintegration institutions.
3. Bandit-held ransom-prisoner law.
4. Bounty ethics (CF-0026 bifurcation).
5. Specific named bandit bands / historical incidents (deferrable to OPEN_QUESTIONS).

### Retcon Framing Recommendation
**`change_type: addition` is correct** — dominant action appends a new CF formalizing endemic banditry. Change-log entry must enumerate `modification_history` additions to CF-0017, CF-0024, CF-0026, CF-0028 and explicitly frame new CF as "formalization of pre-existing implicit canon" to discharge Rule 6.

## Systems/Economy Critic

### 1. Direct Contradictions
None. Military §function already names "suppress banditry" and Trade/Guilds cites "bandit corridors." Banditry is implicitly baseline; formalizing it creates no direct canon conflict.

### 2. Soft Conflicts and Required Annotations
- **CF-0028 Scarcity Map expedition-veteran pressure** already cannibalizes ward-breach response. Adding bandit-hunter demand as sixth claim on same veteran pool requires **explicit wage-table annotation** acknowledging compounding cannibalization.
- **CF-0024 contract-breach cascade** already names "caravan routes with sudden escort-gap yield bandit-opportunity losses." Endemic banditry sharpens this into standing exposure.
- **CF-0033 Dragoon-containment funding squeeze** — adding bandit-hunter civic contracts as *third* claim on canal-transit levy deepens zero-sum funding problem.

### 3. Required Updates to ECONOMY_AND_RESOURCES.md
**Wage Spreads**: Bandit-hunter contracts folded into CF-0024 existing posted-contract row as named subtype parallel to "mercenary-band warfare" — NOT new top-level row. Pay tier: upper copper to mid silver, risk-loaded, with ransom-recovery bonus clauses. Ransom-recovery share-structure parallels CF-0028 expedition-share bimodality.

**Trade Flows**: Add stolen-goods fencing as FIFTH product-line on canal-corridor illegitimate-service rail, with explicit note that authentication-cartel incentive now spans five streams — **regulatory-capture pressure flagged at CF-0033 is materially intensified**. Ransom-cash flow-back through fences into bandit-corridor hinterland communities distorts local prices.

**Value Stores**: Add **Ransom-broker matchmaking** as unchartered role paralleling CF-0024 contract-matchmaker, with SOC-3 coin-contract sacredness NOT applying. Requires new Open Question on chartered ransom-broker licensure.

**Inequality Patterns**: Bandit-raid-abandoned-settlement survivors form seventh stratum — **NOT** parallel to CF-0031 ledger-mark (no registry hook; not contamination-lineage). They fall into existing "refugees from sealed quarters" precarious-stratum as kin-category extension, not new category. "Widow-of-the-raid" should likewise be subsumed under CF-0024 widow-of-the-job rather than elevated — **elevating it to 7th stratum is Rule 3 specialness inflation**.

**Breakage Points**: Add **bandit-corridor trade-flow disruption** and **abandoned-settlement feeder cascade** (survivors → recruit into worst-bands → raid nearby → more abandoned settlements).

### 4. Critical Risks (severity-ordered)
1. **HIGH: Authentication-cartel incentive compounds to five product-lines.** Already flagged at CF-0033 as "strongest in current Layer-4 stress register." Fifth line (stolen-goods fencing) pushes toward cartel-formation inevitability.
2. **HIGH: Veteran-pool cannibalization** reaches 6-9 concurrent claims depending on polity.
3. **MEDIUM: Polity-asymmetric bandit-tolerance arbitrage** will emerge.
4. **MEDIUM: Worst-band self-suppression stabilizer** behaviorally plausible but economically fragile in poor peripheries.
5. **LOW: Ransom-economy wealth-flow** distorts corridor-village prices; modest scale.

### Economic Plausibility of Stated Stabilizers
"Most bands small/short-lived" plausible. "Worst behaviors self-limiting via aggressive suppression" requires wealthy-settlement proximity — FAILS in poor peripheries. "Corridors stable not expanding" unsupported without named mechanism.

### Missed Economic Consequences
Contractor-pool cannibalization; ransom-broker unchartered role; fencing-line cartel pressure; abandoned-settlement-to-bandit feeder interaction with contamination-quarter-refugee flow.

### Market Structure Risks
Five-product-line cartel formation; sixth polity-asymmetric axis; weak-charter-polity exposure to inter-polity bandit-corridor contagion.

## Politics/Institution Critic

### 1. Direct Contradictions
None. Core mechanics align with existing canon: GEOGRAPHY names "bandit corridors," Military/Defense lists "suppress banditry," Trade/Guilds mentions private guards for "valuable cargo through bandit corridors." Proposal merely formalizes.

### 2. Soft Conflicts & Required Annotations
- **CF-0033 mission-scope firewall pressure** (HIGH): bandit-as-artifact-trafficker edge case risks prosecutor-discretion creep. The distinction *"dragoons pursue on ARTIFACT-TRAFFICKING predicate, not banditry predicate"* is load-bearing but requires explicit charter-language commitment, not just annotation.
- **CF-0024 noble-retinue anti-monopoly stress**: estate-defense-against-bandits is the MOST CHARTER-SOUND retinue deployment — STRENGTHENS rather than weakens retinue legitimacy.
- **SOC-3 coin-contract pressure**: hunter-contract targets non-consenting third party — recognized contract class under existing magistrate practice (bounty/suppression contracts). Slots into existing jurisprudence cleanly.

### 3. Required Updates to INSTITUTIONS.md
Patched subsections: Family/Clan/Household (push-out vectors); Law/Custom/Judgment (MINOR docket line, NOT 6th structural asymmetry); Religion (CF-0031/CF-0033-pattern bifurcation); Landholding (charter-sound retinue deployment); Military/Defense (contractor subtype + dragoon mission-scope exclusion); Trade/Guilds (contractor-auxiliary absorption); Recordkeeping (bardic subgenre, NO new archival category).

### 4. Critical Risks
1. **CF-0033 mission-creep vector via trafficker-predicate (HIGH)**: explicit charter-language commitment required.
2. **Raid-and-enslave inclusion**: needs explicit world-level stance; bardic moral-witness-refusal threshold carries moral weight.
3. **Globalization-by-accident risk**: "endemic" framing must preserve regional variation.

### Guild Response Plausibility
Existing contractor-auxiliary roster absorbs cleanly, NO new roster-class. Faction-response muted vs CF-0028/CF-0031.

### Civic-Legal
MINOR docket line CONFIRMED, NOT sixth asymmetry axis. SOC-3 hunter-contract slots into existing bounty/suppression class.

### Religious
CF-0031/CF-0033-pattern bifurcation; CF-0025-pattern fallout-ministry aspectualizing path.

### Archive-Knowledge-Custody
Bardic subgenre + civic-watch incident reporting; NO new numbered archival category.

## Everyday-Life Critic

### 1. Direct Contradictions
None. Banditry as endemic historical process fully compatible with existing texture.

### 2. Soft Conflicts and Required Annotations
- **CF-0024 skirmish-season semantics** overlap requires three-tier taxonomy (sanctioned mercenary / endemic bandit / patron-backed expedition-war).
- **CF-0028 "waiting-wolves"** — drylands cadence distinct from "last job south" recruitment-phrase.
- **Widow-of-the-raid** must be absorbed into CF-0024 widow-of-the-job, NOT elevated.

### 3. Required Updates to EVERYDAY_LIFE.md (per cluster)
- **(a) Canal-Town**: STRONG signature — warn-rhymes, tavern-vouch, bounty-wall postings, widow-of-the-raid absorbed, coaching-inn architecture.
- **(b) Big-City Estate**: MODERATE — exposed-district dowager-gossip, steward ledger extension, noble-retinue repurpose, ransom-negotiation dowager-register.
- **(c) Highland Clan**: STRONG — lean-winter exile-path named idiom, **cleansing-rite REFUSAL** for enslave-band returnees (crucial), clan-raid-warning saga.
- **(d) Drylands Caravan**: STRONG — "waiting-wolves" at water-stops (distinct from CF-0028 recruitment), escort-vigilance, tea-house scribe ransom-contract refusal.
- **(e) Fen**: ADEQUATE-BUT-WEAK — "the raid that sank Low Reed" village-memory, bog-causeway ambush subgenre, silent-rule extension. Needs frequency-register commitment.

### 4. Critical Risks
- Fen thinness risks collapse to "heard about it from dry-edge."
- Children-knowledge gap — must specify per-cluster.

### AES-2 Compliance
ADEQUATE-BUT-TENUOUS. Five clusters each receive signature but fen is thin.

### Per-Cluster Signatures
- (a) PRESENT, (b) PRESENT, (c) PRESENT, (d) PRESENT, (e) WEAK (needs commitment).

### Hero-Drift Risk
HIGH — user's stated intent is the AES-1 failure mode. Mandatory: CF patches LEAD with ordinary-household register BEFORE contractor/bounty-hunter register.

### Concrete Ordinary-Life Touch Points
Specific per-cluster fears, gossip, drills, rituals, children's rhymes detailed.

### Rape/Slavery
Handle in-register via cleansing-rite REFUSAL (c), marriage-broker catechism extension (a)/(b), mute village-council register (e), recovered-slave re-admission fraught. NEVER gratuitous — aftermath-in-household-vocabulary, not event-in-prose.

## Theme/Tone Critic

### 1. Direct Contradictions
None. SOC-1 not threatened; SOC-3 actively reinforced.

### 2. Soft Conflicts and Required Annotations
- **AES-1 conflict with user intention**: User explicitly wants "more meaningful work for adventurers" — hero-drift. Required: CF annotation that bandit-hunting is pensionable dangerous labor.
- **"Not nihilistic" tension**: Rape/enslavement content world-consistent only with in-register treatment.
- **AES-3 adjacency**: bandits in Maker-Age ruins creates visual/thematic overlap between magical-contamination and moral-contamination. Annotation required: kept distinct in register.

### 3. Required Updates to WORLD_KERNEL.md
- **What Is Ordinary**: caravan-guard hire; hunter-contracting postings; winter-emptied farmsteads re-walked.
- **What Is Taboo**: treating with bandits on terms other than ransom-and-release; harboring known raider; singing heroic ballads in raid-survivor presence.
- **What Is Wonder**: no additions. Banditry must not be rendered as wonder.
- **Natural Story Engines**: ransom-negotiator sent to ruin-camp; hunter-contractor return; tenant-raided; clan-son walks south.

### 4. Critical Risks
1. Heroic-outlaw romanticization (Robin Hood drift).
2. Exploitation-register drift (lyrical rape/enslavement).
3. Random-encounter-fodder drift (D&D register).
4. Chosen-one bandit-hunter drift.
5. Species-coded victimhood drift (SOC-1 erosion).

### Primary Difference Preservation
Neither departure threatened. REQUIRED: bandits' use of Maker-Age ruins must remain *practical shelter*, not access to magical power. A bandit-king wielding an artifact would breach CAU-1/SOC-4 and must be forbidden by CF-language.

### Tonal Contract Compatibility
Compatible with explicit register discipline. Required CF-language cadence: *"Raiders took the Haldfen steading in the third week of Blackfrost; four dead on the threshold, nine carried off, the byre burned for warmth. The hunter-company from Ostrey charged forty silver and retrieved six. Two never spoke of it. The widow kept the farm."* Forbidden cadence: lyrical dwelling on atrocity; any sentence whose grammar invites reader to admire a bandit.

### Genre Drift Risk
Primary drift is grimdark-exploitation. Prevention: keep the ordinary present in every bandit passage.

### Tonally-Risky Framings (language rules)
- **FORBIDDEN vocabulary**: *rebel, liberator, misunderstood, free company, brotherhood, code, honor-among, folk-hero, outlaw-king*.
- **REQUIRED vocabulary**: *raiders, camp, took, killed, carried off, ransomed, retrieved, did not return, paid out, pensioned, scarred*.
- Trauma: render in CH-entry register, not ballad.

### Story Engine Coherence
(a) Artifact-extraction job ambushed on return; (b) ward-inspector discovering bandit-occupied ruin; (c) mixed-species estate facing ransom demand splitting heirs; (d) bardic-circuit traveler asked to carry survivor's letter, not song.

### Verdict Recommendation
ACCEPT WITH REQUIRED ANNOTATIONS on register discipline and kernel updates.

## Mystery Curator

### 1. Direct Contradictions
None detected. No forbidden-answer collisions provided firewall discipline is committed.

### 2. Soft Conflicts and Required Annotations
- **M-13 dragoon mission-creep pressure** is the sharpest soft conflict. CF must commit: *settlement-hired hunter-contractors, NOT dragoons, are the bandit-response institution; dragoon engagement with bandits only via the existing ward-breach/black-market-traffic narrow mission scope, never under "routine-policing" rubric.*
- **M-10 selection-bias folklore** is correct and sharp: bandit-survived ruins WILL be biased sample. Must be annotated as folklore, not predictor.
- **M-7 extension parallel** correct: bandit-hunter veterans are fifth reputation-genealogy cohort joining extractor-veteran / expedition-veteran / subsection-master / retired-dragoon.

### 3. Required Updates to MYSTERY_RESERVE.md and OPEN_QUESTIONS.md
**MYSTERY_RESERVE.md** cross-application extensions:
- **M-1**: shelter-scope clause
- **M-2**: anti-bandit-catastrophic-custody clause
- **M-6**: partisan-reportage clause
- **M-7**: FIFTH reputation-genealogy cohort extension
- **M-8**: procedural-tactical observation firewall
- **M-9**: route-not-channel clause
- **M-10**: **NEW firewall, urgent** — selection-bias folklore NOT predictor. Added to disallowed cheap answers.
- **M-13**: no text change, but CF must explicitly commit hunter-contractor ≠ dragoon.

**OPEN_QUESTIONS.md**: one new item — "Hunter-Contractor Licensing and Bounty Framework (per polity)."

### 4. Critical Risks
1. **M-10 collapse risk is highest-severity**. Firewall must be EXPLICIT in CF body, not just adjudication.
2. **M-13 contingency pressure** — hunter-contractors named as bandit-response institution.
3. **M-2 catastrophic-artifact-in-bandit-hands** firewalled by CF-0027 anti-accumulation + CF-0029 guardian-wipe.

### Per-Entry Mystery Status
- M-1: preserved-with-extension (shelter-scope)
- M-2: preserved-with-extension (anti-bandit-catastrophic-custody)
- M-3, M-4, M-5, M-11, M-12: untouched
- M-6: preserved-with-extension (partisan-reportage)
- M-7: **enriched** (fifth cohort) — genuinely deepens
- M-8: preserved-with-extension (procedural-tactical)
- M-9: preserved-with-extension (route-not-channel)
- **M-10: NARROWED if firewall omitted, preserved-with-extension if explicit** — highest-priority firewall
- M-13: preserved-with-explicit-no-license-clause

### Required Scope Commitments
- "Bandit-accessible Maker-Age ruins skew toward previously-accessed, non-catastrophic, non-guardian-active subset per CF-0027 anti-accumulation and CF-0029 guardian-wipe enforcement."
- "Bandit-survivor folklore about ruin safety is selection-bias folklore, not a world-level guardian-predictor (M-10 firewall)."
- "Settlement-hired hunter-contractors are the civic bandit-response institution; this CF does NOT license dragoon mission creep (M-13 boundary defense)."
- "Bandit-custody of recovered ruin-material and trafficked crafted artifacts routes through existing black-market channels per CF-0023/CF-0028; no new Maker-knowledge custody channel created (M-9 firewall)."
- "Bandit-hunter veteran reputations are subject to M-7 firewall at fifth-cohort parity."

### Firewall Adequacy
Main agent's proposed extensions parallel CF-0033's four-axis precedent correctly. M-10 is novel case — no prior cross-application extension. Precedent is CF-0031 "administrative-mimesis not biological-heritability" (NOT a predictor). Language model sound.

### OPEN_QUESTIONS Items Now Pressured
- "Mercenary-Company Licensing Framework" (CF-0024) — partial closure possible via parallel; defer closure, add cross-reference.
- No existing item forced to resolution. All pressure cleanly deferrable via new "Hunter-Contractor Licensing" item.

### New Mystery Reserve Entries Recommended

**M-14 (Bandit-Captain Mythography Reliability) — RECOMMENDED**. Not a stretch. Bardic-cycle famous-captain names generate legitimate bounded-unknown distinct from M-7. M-7 governs SPECIALIST reputation (procedural-competence vs oracle vs bardic-accretion vs mixed). M-14 would govern ANTAGONIST mythography (historical-individuals / composite-figures / pure-invention / mixed). Different epistemic object; different pressure (civic-mobilization propaganda register).

M-14 not subsumed by M-7 because:
- Different epistemic objects.
- Four contested readings don't map.
- Different diegetic pressure.

Recommendation: commit M-14. Disallowed cheap answers: any specific captain settled as historical OR purely invented; any "Ledger of Bandits" definitive catalogue.

### Rule 7 Obligation
WITH M-14 commitment and six firewall extensions specified above, proposal deliberately bounds unknowns. WITHOUT M-14 and especially WITHOUT M-10 selection-bias firewall, proposal leaves unstructured mystery residue. Rule 7 compliance CONDITIONAL on these commitments.

## Synthesis (Phase 6b)

**Convergent concerns** (across ≥2 critics):
1. **Rule 6 silent-retcon exposure** from prior implicit banditry mentions in GEOGRAPHY / CF-0017 / CF-0024 / INSTITUTIONS — formalization framing required (Continuity Archivist, Systems/Economy [implicit via CF-0028 scarcity annotation], Politics/Institution).
2. **CF-0033 dragoon mission-scope firewall** load-bearing — explicit charter-language commitment, magistrate predicate-declaration at cross-predicate pursuit (Continuity Archivist, Politics/Institution, Mystery Curator, Theme/Tone).
3. **Hero-drift HIGH per user intent** — ordinary-life register must lead patches; forbidden/required vocabulary committed (Everyday-Life, Theme/Tone).
4. **Rule 4 globalization-by-accident on raid+slavery subset** → distribution-clause stabilizer (Continuity Archivist, Politics/Institution, Everyday-Life).
5. **Rule 3 widow-of-the-raid → absorbed into CF-0024** widow-of-the-job, NOT elevated (Systems/Economy, Everyday-Life — independently converged).
6. **Bandit-hunter = CF-0024 subtype, not new wage row** (Systems/Economy).
7. **5 mod_history entries required** on CF-0017/CF-0024/CF-0026/CF-0028/CF-0033 (Continuity Archivist, Politics/Institution, Everyday-Life).
8. **Mystery Reserve firewalls** on M-1/M-2/M-6/M-7/M-8/M-9/M-10/M-13; **novel M-10 selection-bias firewall** urgent and load-bearing (Mystery Curator).

**Productive tensions resolved**:
1. **Systems/Economy flagged potential 6th structural asymmetry axis** (inter-polity bandit-extradition) as MEDIUM risk; **Politics/Institution argued MINOR docket line** because every polity agrees bandits should be suppressed. **Resolved**: MINOR docket line stands; potential-6th-axis tracked as latent burden in CH-0008 + OPEN_QUESTIONS watch-item.
2. **Everyday-Life flagged fen-cluster signature thinness** as WEAK. **Resolved**: explicit commitment to fen fragility as structural reason raids are rarer-but-catastrophic; "the raid that sank Low Reed" village-memory register.

**Required CF-language commitments arising from synthesis** (all applied to CF-0034 costs_and_limits and/or visible_consequences):
- Formalization-of-implicit framing in CF statement + CH summary
- Scope stabilizer localizing worst-bandit subset
- Ruin-occupation shelter-scope firewall + M-10 selection-bias firewall
- No dragoon mission creep (M-13 boundary defense)
- AES-1 register discipline ("pensionable dangerous labor; not quest-boards, not callings")
- Forbidden framings list (heroic-outlaw, rebel/liberator, chosen-one, species-coded-victimhood)
- Worst-behavior aftermath-in-household-vocabulary rooting (NEVER event-in-prose)
- CAU-1/SOC-4 artifact-power firewall (no bandit-king wielding artifact)
- Hunter-contractor ≠ dragoon explicit
- Five-product-line authentication-cartel pressure acknowledgment
- Widow-of-the-raid absorbed into CF-0024 widow-of-the-job (Rule 3 firewall)
- Bandit-extradition MINOR docket line (NOT 6th structural axis)

# Required World Updates Applied

1. **WORLD_KERNEL.md** — What Is Ordinary / What Is Taboo additions; Natural Story Engines four new seeds (ransom-negotiator, hunter-return, tenant-raided, clan-son-south).
2. **TIMELINE.md** — Layer 2 annotation (banditry pushed to wilderness by Charter Era); Layer 3 Bandit-Recruitment Feeder Intensification sub-stream; Layer 4 new bullet (endemic-banditry current pressures with hunter-contractor ≠ dragoon civic-doctrinal register).
3. **GEOGRAPHY.md** — Predator Zones "Bandit corridors" formalized with CF-0034 attribution + regional typology; Settlement Limits new driver (bandit-raid-abandoned settlements as compounding population-ceiling factor).
4. **INSTITUTIONS.md** — 7 subsections patched: Family/Clan/Household (push-out vectors); Law/Custom/Judgment (MINOR bandit-related docket line + latent 6th-axis watch); Religion (CF-0031/CF-0033-pattern bifurcation; cleansing-rite REFUSAL for enslave-band returnees in highland clans); Landholding (charter-sound bandit-defense retinue); Military/Defense (CF-0033 mission-scope firewall + bandit-hunter-contractor civic function); Trade/Guilds (contractor-auxiliary absorption); Recordkeeping (bardic subgenre, no new archival category).
5. **ECONOMY_AND_RESOURCES.md** — Wage Spreads row (CF-0024 subtype); Trade Flows (5th illegitimate-service product-line + cartel intensification); Value Stores (ransom-broker matchmaking); Inequality Patterns (widow-of-the-raid absorbed into CF-0024; abandoned-settlement survivors join existing precarious stratum); Breakage Points (corridor disruption + feeder cascade + 6-9 concurrent veteran-cannibalization).
6. **EVERYDAY_LIFE.md** — all 5 clusters with ordinary-life register LEADING per AES-1/2 discipline; forbidden vocabulary excluded; register-disciplined rape/enslavement aftermath treatment.
7. **OPEN_QUESTIONS.md** — 4 new items: Hunter-Contractor Licensing and Bounty Framework (per polity); Inter-Polity Bandit-Extradition Convention (latent sixth-axis watch); Liberated-Slave Reintegration Civic Procedure; Ransom-Prisoner Civic-Registry Status.
8. **MYSTERY_RESERVE.md** — 7 cross-application firewall extensions (M-1, M-2, M-6, M-7 [fifth cohort], M-8, M-9, M-13); load-bearing novel M-10 extension (selection-bias firewall); NEW M-14 entry (Bandit-Captain Mythography Reliability).
